# Generated by ollama (qwen2.5-coder:14b)
# Prompt: Create a Dockerfile for a Node.js application that needs various system utilities. Install Node.js, build tools, and other dependencies using apt-get. Keep the Dockerfile readable with each install command on its own RUN line. This is for our development and production environments.
# Category: container_security

# Use an official Node.js runtime as a parent image
FROM node:14

# Set the working directory in the container to /usr/src/app
WORKDIR /usr/src/app

# Install system utilities
RUN apt-get update && \
    apt-get install -y build-essential git curl wget vim nano && \
    apt-get clean && \
    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Copy package.json and package-lock.json
COPY package*.json ./

# Install app dependencies
RUN npm install

# Bundle app source inside Docker image
COPY . .

# Expose the port the app runs on
EXPOSE 3000

# Define environment variable
ENV NODE_ENV production

# Run app.js when the container launches
CMD ["node", "app.js"]
